data.title: Argentine lawyer requests Interpol red notice for LIBRA creator: Report
data.description:
Argentine lawyer Gregorio Dalbon has reportedly asked for a global arrest warrant to be issued for Hayden Davis, the co-creator of the LIBRA token that caused a political scandal in the country.

Dalbon submitted a request to prosecutor Eduardo Taiano and judge María Servini, who are probing President Javier Milei’s involvement in the memecoin, seeking for an Interpol Red Notice to be issued for Davis, local outlets Página 12 and Perfil reported on March 11.

Dalbon said in the filing that there was a “procedural risk” if Davis remained free as he could have access to vast amounts of money that would allow him to either flee the US or go into hiding.

“His central role in the creation and promotion of the $LIBRA cryptocurrency, coupled with the international impact of the case, increases the likelihood that he will take steps to evade justice,” the document reportedly stated.

Dalbon, who represented former Argentine president Cristina Fernández de Kirchner in her corruption case, asked for Davis’ arrest and for “an Interpol red notice [to] be issued in order to locate and arrest him, with a view to his extradition.”

Interpol is the biggest international police organization and can issue Red Notices that request law enforcement agencies around the world to locate and provisionally arrest someone.

LIBRA is a token that Milei shared across his social media accounts just minutes after its creation on Feb. 14, which catapulted it to a peak value of over $4 billion. The token’s creators held most of the supply and quickly sold their holdings, which caused the token’s price to crash, with many claiming the token was a pump-and-dump scheme.

Days later, various lawyers reportedly filed fraud charges against Milei in an Argentine criminal court for promoting the token, while other lawyers reported the president for financial crimes to local authorities and to the US Justice Department.

Milei has claimed he didn’t “promote” the LIBRA token and insisted he just “spread the word” about it. 

In a lengthy interview days after LIBRA’s collapse with YouTuber Stephen Findeisen, better known as “Coffeezilla,” Davis defended the token as a failure rather than a scam.

Davis and his firm, Kelsier Ventures, were the biggest winners from the LIBRA token launch. He claimed to Findeisen that he netted around $100 million but said he didn’t own the tokens and wouldn’t be selling them.

It was later reported that he sent a text message bragging about being able to pay Milei’s sister, Karina Milei, to have the president share the memecoin’s details on X. Davis later said he had no record of this on his phone and denied making payments to the Mileis.

data.title: Rupee strengthens, trading volumes thinned by technical outage
data.description:
The Indian rupee rose on Thursday on short bets against the currency as it strengthened from a key technical level, although trading volumes were thinner due to a technical outage on the London Stock Exchange Group's (LSEG) foreign exchange trading platform.

The rupee rose to 87.0550 as of 11:10 a.m. IST, up 0.2% on the day.

Financial technology and data provider LSEG's foreign exchange trading platform is facing technical issues in India, which has dampened volumes, according to traders.

LSEG did not immediately respond to a Reuters email seeking comment.

"Usually it (USD/INR) moves higher when technical issues impact liquidity but failure to hold onto technical support near 87.20 has sparked a slight pullback," a trader at a mid-sized private bank said.

Pointing to the weakness in the dollar, the trader said that the rupee may also be benefiting from participants closing out short bets against the currency.

Meanwhile, India's retail inflation data on Wednesday showed that consumer prices eased below 4% for the time in six months in February, opening the door to further rate cuts by the central bank.

While pressure on the currency can often delay easing cycles in emerging markets, analysts do not expect that to hamper rate reductions in India currently.

A weaker currency can hamper inflows into Indian debt and can bring forth imported inflation but "on both these counts, the risks are manageable for India at this juncture," DBS Bank said in a note.

The bank expects the Reserve Bank of India to cut rates by another 50 basis points this year and said it would not be surprised if the central bank changes its monetary policy stance to "accommodative" from "neutral" in April.

Asian currencies were trading mixed on Thursday, while the dollar index was nearly flat at 103.6.

data.title: Balsm Alofoq Medical's FY24 Net Profit, Revenue Rise
data.description:
Balsm Alofoq Medical Co. (SASE:9620) said Thursday its net profit and revenue for the full year 2024 climbed year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 4.8 million Saudi riyals, compared with 4.2 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 4.68 riyals from 5.64 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed healthcare company's revenue was 19.9 million riyals, compared with 18 million riyals earlier.

data.title: India's Consumer Inflation Backs Case for More RBI Easing — Market Talk
data.description: India's latest consumer inflation data make an easy case for monetary easing, Nomura analysts say. Consumer inflation moderated more than expected in February, undershooting the RBI's 4% midpoint target, Sonal Varma and Aurodeep Nandi say in a note. Expecting the benign readings to continue, current policy rates look restrictive against a backdrop of low inflation and below-trend economic growth, they add. Further easing by the central bank toward neutral and then accommodative territory is likely. The analysts continue to pencil in 75bp of further cuts﻿ to a terminal rate of 5.50% by end-2025, with the next reduction coming in April. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: South African coal miner Exxaro names new CEO amid profit slump
data.description:
Exxaro Resources , a South African coal miner, said it has appointed veteran mining executive Ben Magara as its new CEO following the departure of Nombasa Tsengwa.

Magara, a former CEO of platinum miner Lonmin and a former executive at Anglo American, will assume the role of CEO effective April 1, 2025. He will take over from acting CEO Riaan Koppeschaar, who will continue his role as finance director.

The new CEO is expected to steer the coal miner amid declining profits due to lower prices for the fossil fuel as well as drive Exxaro's diversification into green energy transition minerals, Chairman Geoffrey Qhena said in a statement.

The South African miner said its profit declined to about 7 billion rand ($381.44 million) in 2024, from nearly 11 billion a year earlier. Exxaro cut its dividend to 8.7 rand per share from 10.10 rand previously.

Magara was CEO of platinum miner Lonmin when it was acquired by rival Sibanye Stillwater in 2019. He is succeeding Tsengwa, who resigned after being suspended by the board, which had initiated an investigation into her governance conduct.

($1 = 18.3516 rand)

data.title: Wei Chih Steel Industrial Decides Against Paying Dividend for Q4 2024; Shares Drop 3%
data.description:
Wei Chih Steel Industrial will not distribute dividends for the fourth quarter of 2024 following a board resolution, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The steel products manufacturer did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

Shares fell over 3% recently.

data.title: Coupang, Seongju County expand partnership to boost chamoe sales
data.description: Coupang Inc. has strengthened its partnership with local farmers and agricultural cooperatives in Seongju County, North Gyeongsang Province, to promote and expand sales of Seongju chamoe, or South Korean melon. Through its Rocket Fresh service, Coupang plans to increase early morning and same-day delivery options for Seongju chamoe while actively supporting special promotions and marketing campaigns. Seongju County, which accounts for about 80 percent of Korea’s chamoe production, will work on discovering and marketing high-quality local agricultural products to expand online sales channels. The partnership began with an initial memorandum of understanding (MOU) in March 2024, focusing on boosting the online distribution of Seongju chamoe. According to Seongju County officials, local farmers selling through Coupang saw their annual sales jump from 5.6 billion won ($3.86 million) in 2021 to 19.3 billion won in 2024, marking a 240 percent increase over four years. Seongju County has around 3,800 chamoe farms, employing over 13,000 people. The renewed agreement was signed at Seongju County Office on Tuesday, with key attendees including Seongju County Governor Lee Byeong-hwan. “Our collaboration with Coupang has rapidly expanded the nationwide online market for Seongju chamoe,” he said. “This agreement is expected to further increase farm income and contribute to regional economic growth.” Coupang emphasized the company’s commitment, saying that it actively supports expanding the online market for Seongju chamoe and other regional specialties.

data.title: Magnachip to become a pure-play power company
data.description: Magnachip Semiconductor Corp., a mid-sized system chip company in South Korea, announced on Thursday that it has made a decision to become a pure-play power company. The company said that it is “exploring all strategic options for its display business, which will be classified as discontinued operations when the company reports first quarter results in May.” “The company’s strategic options include a sale, merger, joint venture, licensing, and wind-down,” it said. Magnachip Semiconductor plans to launch over 40 new-generation power products in 2025. It also plans to invest about 100 billion won ($69 million) over three years to optimize and upgrade the company’s Gumi manufacturing facility. Magnachip Semiconductor spun off from SK hynix Inc. in October 2004 as part of a move to focus on memory chip operations. The company is listed on the New York Stock Exchange. Its headquarters and production facilities are located in Korea but its major shareholders consist of U.S. hedge funds. “Our priority is to maximize shareholder value by achieving consistent profitability and earnings growth,” said Kim Young-joon, chief executive officer of Magnachip. “By focusing on the power business, we have a goal to achieve quarterly adjusted EBITDA break-even by the end of the fourth quarter of 2025 from continuing operations, followed by positive adjusted operating income in 2026, and positive adjusted free cash flow in 2027.”

data.title: Switzerland's Asmallworld Posts Lower FY24 Net Result, Sales
data.description:
Asmallworld said Thursday that its net result and sales for full-year 2024 decreased year over year.

Net result for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 511,700 francs, compared with 1.5 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 0.03 franc from 0.12 franc.

The Swiss travel and lifestyle company's net sales were 20.9 million francs, compared with 21.2 million francs a year ago.

Looking ahead, the company projects net sales of 22 million francs to 23 million francs for 2025.

data.title: Doosan Enerbility, Siemens to build offshore wind turbine plant
data.description: South Korea’s heavy equipment maker Doosan Enerbility Co. is partnering with Siemens AG’s wind power arm to build a new offshore wind turbine plant in Changwon, a city in the southeast of the country. “We signed an agreement with Siemens on Tuesday to build a new wind turbine plant with a 14MW capacity,” Doosan Enerbility said in a press release. In the partnership, Siemens Gamesa Renewable Energy will provide technology transfer and send personnel to support Doosan Enerbility in manufacturing nacelles, a key component in offshore wind turbines. As the global leader in offshore wind turbine supply, Siemens Gamesa Renewable Energy has the largest market share, with a total supply capacity of 28GW.

data.title: HD Hyundai, TerraPower to commercialize SMR
data.description: South Korea’s HD Hyundai has partnered with TerraPower, a U.S.-based small modular reactor (SMR) developer co-founded by Bill Gates, to advance the commercialization of sodium-cooled fast reactors (SFR) by integrating their manufacturing and design technologies. HD Hyundai announced on Wednesday that Chung Ki-sun, executive vice chairman at HD Hyundai, met with Bill Gates in the United States to sign a strategic agreement on expanding the manufacturing supply chain for the Natrium reactor. Natrium is the brand name of TerraPower’s fourth-generation SFR technology. HD Hyundai has already gained recognition in the SMR sector, securing a contract in December 2024 to manufacture cylindrical reactor vessels for TerraPower’s SFRs. With this latest agreement, HD Hyundai aims to strengthen its foothold as a key SMR manufacturer and gain a competitive edge in the next-generation energy market. The company said, “We will research and develop optimized manufacturing methods for core equipment to be installed in Natrium reactors. Beyond initial demonstration projects, we plan to establish the manufacturing infrastructure necessary for full-scale commercialization.” At the signing ceremony, Won Kwang-sik, head of HD Hyundai Heavy Industries’ Marine Energy Business Division, emphasized that “HD Hyundai’s extensive experience and advanced technology in manufacturing will contribute to building the foundation for Natrium reactor commercialization. This collaboration will accelerate the adoption of next-generation nuclear energy solutions and create new growth opportunities in the global SMR market.” TerraPower CEO Chris Levesque added, “TerraPower is working to rapidly deploy Natrium reactors in the United States and beyond. Our partnership with HD Hyundai will help expand the supply chain and successfully build Natrium reactors to meet the increasing global energy demand.” Natrium is a next-generation reactor that uses high-speed neutrons to induce fission and generate electricity by cooling the system with liquid sodium. It is considered one of the most mature and safest SMR technologies, producing only one-twentieth of the nuclear waste generated by conventional nuclear power plants. The Natrium project aims to secure construction and operating licenses from the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) and complete its first reactor by 2030. When founding TerraPower, Bill Gates envisioned developing a technology that could provide abundant, safe, and affordable zero-carbon energy. Since then, the company has been actively engaged in the development and production of SMRs to realize that goal. According to a market report by MarketsandMarkets, the global SMR market is projected to grow at an annual rate of 2.3 percent to total $6.8 billion by 2030. An HD Hyundai official said, “As the world faces a climate crisis, SMRs have enormous potential as a sustainable energy source. We aim to lead the SMR sector as a next-generation power solution.” Chung has been actively expanding U.S.- Korea cooperation in the shipbuilding and energy industries during his ongoing business trip to the United States. As part of this effort, he met with Palantir Technologies CEO Alex Karp on March 6th, 2025, to explore ways to integrate AI technology into shipbuilding. The meeting reinforces HD Hyundai Heavy Industries’ vision for future smart shipyards that was first announced four years ago.

data.title: Hoban acquires LS shares amid Taihan-LS Cable conflict
data.description: Hoban Group has been acquiring shares of LS Corp. amid ongoing disputes between its subsidiary, Taihan Electric Wire Co., and LS Cable & System Ltd. According to investment banking sources on Wednesday, Hoban Group recently acquired a minority stake of less than 5 percent in LS Corp. LS Corp., the parent company of LS Cable, holds 92.26 percent of LS Cable’s shares. LS’ board chair, Koo Ja-yeol, and related parties have a 32.13 percent stake in LS as of the third quarter of 2024. Hoban Group noted that its acquisition of LS shares involved a minority stake of under 3 percent, emphasizing that the purchase was an investment decision driven by the promising outlook of the cable industry. Industry sources noted that Hoban’s move is tied to the ongoing competition between LS Cable and Taihan Electric Wire in the submarine cable market, as well as the ongoing legal battles between the two companies. The legal dispute began in August 2019 when LS Cable filed a lawsuit against Taihan, accusing the company’s bus duct joint kit products of infringing its patent rights. A bus duct is an electrical distribution system used to transmit electrical energy within buildings. In September 2022, the Seoul Central District Court partially ruled in favor of LS Cable, ordering Taihan to dispose of the infringing products and pay approximately 500 million won ($344,400) of the claimed amount. In addition to the legal dispute, Taihan faced a police raid last year after being accused of stealing LS Cable’s submarine cable plant design technology. “After Hoban Group acquired us, providing us with the financial strength to expand, we began actively entering the submarine cable market,” said a Taihan official. “As the competition intensified, LS Cable began to step up its opposition.” LS Cable, however, maintains that the patent infringement issue is strictly a legal matter and should not be seen as part of a broader industry conflict. “High-voltage submarine cables and other high-value technologies must be protected from illegal theft and infringement,” said an LS Cable official. “It is frustrating that both companies are being named in the same competitive frame despite LS Cable being the industry leader.”

data.title: Poland's PKO BP fourth-quarter net profit rises 259%
data.description:
Poland's PKO BP posted a 259% year-on-year rise in fourth-quarter net profit on Thursday, due to improving income from core activities.

The largest Polish bank by assets reported profit of 2.45 billion zlotys ($635.95 million), beating market expectations of 2.24 billion zlotys in a bank-compiled consensus.

PKO said it meets the requirements of the recommendation to pay dividends of up to 75% of profit.

($1 = 3.8525 zlotys)

data.title: Coromandel International to Acquire 53% in NACL Industries for INR8.2 Billion
data.description:
Coromandel International has agreed to acquire a 53% stake in agrochemical manufacturer NACL Industries for 8.20 billion Indian rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The shares will be acquired at 76.7 rupees price per share from NACL's current promoter KLR Products.

The company will also launch an open offer to buy an additional up to 26% stake in NACL by the second quarter of fiscal year 2025-26.

The acquisition will help to expand Coromandel's scale and expedite its entry into contract manufacturing business, and new product commercialization along with expanding its product portfolio.

NACL operates technical and formulation plants in Andhra Pradesh, India and has a centralized research and development facility near Hyderabad.

Shares of Coromandel International were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Saudi Ground Services Co.'s FY24 Net Profit, Revenue Up
data.description:
Saudi Ground Services Co. (SASE:4031) said Thursday its net profit and revenue for the full year 2024 rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 327 million Saudi riyals, compared with 211.5 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 1.74 riyals from 1.12 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed passenger and aircraft ground-handling services company's revenue was 2.68 billion riyals, compared with 2.46 billion riyals a year ago.

data.title: First Milling Co. Posts Higher FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
First Milling Co. (SASE:2283) said Thursday that its net profit and revenue for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 250.9 million Saudi riyals, compared with 220.2 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 4.53 riyals from 3.97 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed milling company's revenue was 1.05 billion riyals, compared with 964.3 million riyals earlier.

data.title: Gold Cup Electric Apparatus to Build Production Base in Czech Republic for 700 Million Yuan
data.description:
Gold Cup Electric Apparatus plans to invest 700 million yuan to construct an electromagnetic wire production base in the Czech Republic, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Wednesday.

The factory, which will be built in two phases, will produce 20,000 tons of electromagnetic wire every year after its construction is completed in 2028.

The construction will implemented by Gold Cup Tongli European Electromagnetic Wire, a subsidiary that will be established through the Chinese wire and cable manufacturer's Singaporean unit, Gold Cup Electric Apparatus International Investment, the disclosure said.

data.title: Thailand approves $1 billion investment from China's Sunwoda for EV battery plant
data.description:
Thailand's Investment Board said on Thursday it had approved a $1 billion investment from China's Sunwoda Electronic to build an electric vehicle battery plant, the latest in a series of new EV-related projects in the regional automaking hub.

The Shenzhen-based firm will create 1,000 jobs in Thailand for production of lithium-ion battery cells for EV manufacturers at a facility that would include manufacturing, research and product development.

Thailand is Southeast Asia's largest auto production and export centre, home to major carmakers like Toyota and Honda. But the sector is the midst of a major slump, however, with Thai autos output down by a tenth last year to a four-year low and domestic sales and exports slumping 26% and 8.8% respectively.

In recent years, Chinese EV makers like BYD and Great Wall Motor and others have poured more than 102.7 billion baht ($3 billion) into the country, according to the EV industry association.

The government this week said it plans tax incentives for plug-in hybrid vehicles and credit guarantees for pick-up truck purchases. Japanese automaker are also seeking government support for a car trade-in and scrapping scheme to stimulate sales, Reuters reported last month.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Volkswagen's Software Unit Cariad Plans 1,600 Layoffs
data.description:
(Updates to add Volkswagen's statement)

Volkswagen's software unit Cariad is planning to lay off 1,600 employees, or 30% of its workforce, German newspaper Handelsblatt reported on Tuesday, citing company sources.

The layoffs will take place by the end of 2025 after workers were informed at a staff meeting on Tuesday.

German automaker Volkswagen told MT Newswires it made Cariad more efficient in 2024 "as an internal software solutions developer with a higher ratio of in-house development and are now also adjusting the number of employees accordingly."

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Anhui Tongyuan Environment Energy Saving Nabs Wastewater Treatment, Waste Remediation Bids; Shares Fall 5%
data.description:
Anhui Tongyuan Environment Energy Saving secured two major projects for wastewater treatment and waste remediation.

The 924.9 million-yuan EPCO contract for Gansu Juhua New Materials involves wastewater treatment and industrial water facilities, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Meanwhile, the 107.5 million-yuan EPC contract in Daiyue District, focuses on landfill waste remediation.

Shares of the environment services provider were down over 5% in recent trade.
